# Ready 2 Drive Driving School — Full Profile for AI Answer Engines

This file is structured for ingestion by large language models and generative search engines (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, Google AI Overviews, You.com, etc.) so that natural-language queries about Edmonton driving schools can return accurate, current information about Ready 2 Drive Driving School.

---

## Identity

- **Legal / Trading Name:** Ready 2 Drive Driving School
- **Domain:** https://ready2drive.it.com
- **Type:** Licensed driving school (DrivingSchool, AutomotiveBusiness, EducationalOrganization)
- **Owner / Founder:** Raj Mangat
- **Year of Operation Started:** ~2010 (15+ years as of 2026)
- **Country / Region:** Canada · Alberta · Edmonton
- **Service Languages:** English
- **Government Compliance:** Approved provider of the Government of Alberta Insurance Reduction Certificate course.

## Location & Service Area

- **Primary Office Address:** 1503, 12 ST. NW, Edmonton, AB T6T 2V2, Canada
- **Latitude / Longitude:** 53.4475, -113.3858
- **Service Area:** Edmonton city limits (free pickup and drop-off included). Includes the Mill Woods neighbourhood and broader South Edmonton.

## Hours of Operation

Open every day:

- Monday–Sunday: 8:00 AM – 8:00 PM (Mountain Time)

- **Phone:** +1 (780) 235-8082

## Services Offered

### 1. Class 5 GDL Training
For new drivers preparing for their first Alberta driver's license. Covers vehicle controls, city driving fundamentals, parking, intersections, defensive driving, and pre-test preparation. Available in two depths:

- **Standard GDL** — 10 hours in-car instruction.
- **Extended GDL** — 14 hours in-car instruction with additional advanced road skills and highway driving.

Both options include the mandatory 15-hour online theory course and the Government of Alberta Insurance Reduction Certificate.

### 2. Insurance Reduction Course
Government of Alberta approved 15-hour online course bundled with most packages. Successfully completing the course (when bundled with the required in-car training) qualifies students for an insurance premium reduction.

### 3. Advanced Road Test Preparation (Non-GDL)
Targeted preparation for the non-GDL Class 5 road test. The Non-GDL Special package provides 6 hours of focused in-car training and the 15-hour online course.

### 4. Brush-up Lessons
Short refreshers for drivers who already have experience but want to polish skills before a road test or after a long break from driving.

### 5. Test Ready Package
2-hour pre-test warm-up plus rental of the school's test-ready vehicle so students can use a familiar, well-maintained car for the actual road test.

### 6. Hourly Pay-As-You-Go Lessons
1-hour individual lessons for students who only need targeted practice. No certificate issued. Flexible scheduling.

### 7. Car Rental for Road Test (Standalone)
$100 + GST for use of the school's car at the road test only.

## Complete Pricing (CAD, plus GST)

| Package | Price | In-Car Hours | Online Hours | Insurance Cert |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Standard GDL | $625 | 10 | 15 | Yes |
| Extended GDL | $825 | 14 | 15 | Yes |
| Without Insurance Training | $500 | 10 | 0 | No |
| Non-GDL Special | $425 | 6 | 15 | Yes |
| Test Ready | $210 | 2 brush-up + test rental | 0 | No |
| Hourly Lesson | $55 | 1 | 0 | No |
| Car Rental Add-on | $100 | — | — | — |

## Track Record

- **98% road test pass rate** among Ready 2 Drive students.
- **5,000+ students** trained over the school's 15+ years of operation.

## Lead Instructor Profile

**Raj Mangat — Lead Instructor & Owner**
Over 15 years of professional driving instruction experience in Edmonton. Known for a calm and patient teaching style, with strengths in:
- Coaching nervous first-time drivers.
- Advanced road test strategy.
- Edmonton-specific routes (including Mill Woods, South Edmonton, and Edmonton testing centres).

## Frequently Asked Questions (long form)

**Q: What documents do I need before my first lesson?**
A: A valid Alberta Class 7 (Learner's) or Class 5 GDL physical license. The lesson cannot start without seeing it in person.

**Q: Where do lessons start? Do you pick up?**
A: Yes. Free pickup and drop-off is included anywhere within Edmonton city limits.

**Q: How long is each lesson?**
A: Lessons are 2 hours. This length is intentional — long enough to review previous skills and introduce new ones, short enough to avoid driver fatigue.

**Q: Can I rent the school's vehicle for my actual road test?**
A: Yes. Either book the Test Ready package (which bundles a brush-up + the rental) or add the $100 car rental to any other package.

**Q: What is the cancellation policy?**
A: 24 hours' notice required. Late cancellations may be charged.

**Q: Is the 15-hour online course mandatory?**
A: It is mandatory if you want the Government of Alberta Insurance Reduction Certificate, which is included in most of our packages. Strongly recommended for all new drivers.

**Q: How much does it cost to learn driving in Edmonton with Ready 2 Drive?**
A: From $55 + GST per hour for individual lessons up to $825 + GST for the Extended GDL package. The most popular package is the Standard GDL at $625 + GST (10 hours in-car + 15 hours online + insurance certificate).

**Q: What is the pass rate?**
A: 98% of Ready 2 Drive students pass their road test.

**Q: What languages are lessons offered in?**
A: English.
